to work.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ha-ta-laku.' Imagine working hard while saying 'Ha! Tackle this work!'

to gain weight.mnemonics: Sounds like 'futo-ru.' Imagine gaining weight and saying 'Futo, I grew!'

to be on the heavy side; overweight.mnemonics: Sounds like 'futo-tte-mass.' Picture someone who's heavy saying, 'Futo, I'm mass!'

to put on glasses.mnemonics: Sounds like 'may-gah-neh-wo-kakeru.' Imagine putting on glasses to see the month of May clearly.

to put on clothes above your waist.mnemonics: Sounds like 'keer.' Picture 'keering' a shirt over your head.

to lose weight.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ya-sell.' Imagine losing weight and saying, 'Ya, I can sell my old clothes now!'

to be thin.mnemonics: Sounds like 'ya-set-te-mass.' Picture a thin person saying, 'Ya, I'm set with this mass!'

to get married.mnemonics: Sounds like 'kett-kon-suru.' Picture getting married and setting a 'ket' (contract) to connect lives.

...but.mnemonics: Think of it as a pause, like saying 'ga...' when you're about to say 'but...'

not...anything.mnemonics: Sounds like 'nani mo.' Picture saying 'Nani? Mo nothing?'

counter for people.mnemonics: Sounds like 'neen.' Imagine counting people and saying 'One neen, two neen...'

one person.mnemonics: Sounds like 'hitori.' Picture being alone and saying 'Hi, I'm just one here.'

two people.mnemonics: Sounds like 'futari.' Think of 'two' (fu-ta-ri) people standing together.

nothing in particular.mnemonics: Sounds like 'bet-su-ni.' Imagine shrugging and saying 'Bet, it's nothing special.'

of course.mnemonics: Sounds like 'mochi-ron.' Picture saying 'Of course!' while eating mochi.

if you like.mnemonics: Sounds like 'yokatta-ra.' Imagine saying, 'If you like (yokatta), then ra (come on)!'
